name: Urbasa Circular Trail
name_original: Zudaire, Larraona, Bosque encantado de Artea – Ruta Circular
description: Tackle the difficult 26.5-mile Zudaire, Larraona, Enchanted Forest of Artea circular touring cycling route with 2995 feet of gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Améscoa Baja offers diverse landscapes within Navarra, Spain, characterized by the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, the Sierra de Urbasa, and various valleys. The region features serene forests, rocky terrains, and open vistas, with roads and paths winding through its unique environment. The Urederra River, known for its turquoise waters, flows through dense beech forests, providing picturesque scenery near cycling routes. This area combines natural beauty with varied terrain, suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Améscoa Baja The most popular touring cycling route is Urbasa Circular Trail, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the varied terrain of the Urbasa Natural Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cueva de Los Cristinos – Fresh water spring loop from Camping Urbasa, a moderate 23.7 miles (38.1 km) path. This route leads through natural landscapes, including a fresh water spring. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Camping Urbasa, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail leading through the natural surroundings of Camping Urbasa, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Améscoa Baja is defined by the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, the Sierra de Urbasa, and scenic valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Améscoa Baja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Améscoa Baja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja?
A: Touring cycling in Améscoa Baja offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter serene forests, rocky terrains, and open vistas, particularly within the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park and the Sierra de Urbasa. Routes often traverse scenic valleys, combining more technical sections with easier paths through stunning natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja?
A: Yes, Améscoa Baja offers routes suitable for families. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are options that provide gentler rides through scenic valleys. For example, the region has 1 easy route and 7 moderate routes, which might be more suitable for families. Always check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Where can I find circular touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972834403/urbasa-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbasa Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 26.5-mile route exploring the varied terrain of the Urbasa Natural Park. Another great circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40378265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Los Cristinos – Fresh water spring loop from Camping Urbasa&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 23.7-mile path.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja?
A: Améscoa Baja is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Urederra River&lt;/a&gt;, known for its vibrant turquoise waters and captivating waterfalls. Other notable natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portupekoleze Arch&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Los Cristinos&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, offering serene forests and unique geological formations.

Q: Are there any viewpoints accessible by touring bike in Améscoa Baja?
A: Yes, the region offers several stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ubaba Viewpoint (Pilatos Balcony)&lt;/a&gt; is a particularly notable spot, offering breathtaking vistas. You can explore this area on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10523371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ubaba Viewpoint loop from Camping Urbasa&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 29.7-mile ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Améscoa Baja?
A: Améscoa Baja is an excellent destination for touring cycling throughout much of the year. The region's diverse landscapes, including forests and valleys, offer pleasant conditions. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, generally spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the cooler, forested areas.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja?
A: The touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja cater to a range of abilities. While there are 7 moderate routes and 1 easy route, the majority (22 out of 30) are classified as difficult. These challenging routes often involve significant elevation gains, such as the 912 meters of ascent on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972834403/urbasa-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbasa Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, or the 1179 meters on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20522737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkomendi Dolmen – Agurain/Salvatierra loop from Améscoa Baja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Améscoa Baja?
A: The touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. Over 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning natural beauty of the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through mountain passes in Améscoa Baja?
A: Yes, the region includes several mountain passes that can be part of touring cycling routes. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5136015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Lizarraga&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbasa Hairpin Bends&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4071499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PuertoDeAramendia&lt;/a&gt;. These passes often offer challenging climbs and rewarding descents with panoramic views.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Urederra River and its waterfalls?
A: Absolutely. The Urederra River is a highlight of the region, known for its strikingly vibrant turquoise waters and captivating waterfalls, especially at its source. While specific routes directly along the river for touring bikes might be limited due to terrain, many routes in the area, such as those around Baquedano, offer access to this natural treasure. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Urederra River&lt;/a&gt; is a must-see attraction.

Q: Are there options for longer touring cycling adventures in Améscoa Baja?
A: Yes, Améscoa Baja offers routes suitable for longer touring adventures. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20522737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkomendi Dolmen – Agurain/Salvatierra loop from Améscoa Baja&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 45.3-mile (72.8 km) route that takes approximately 4 hours 56 minutes to complete, offering a significant distance and elevation gain for experienced cyclists.

Q: What is the average duration for touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Améscoa Baja varies significantly depending on the distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972834403/urbasa-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbasa Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 28 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40378265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Los Cristinos – Fresh water spring loop from Camping Urbasa&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Longer routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20522737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkomendi Dolmen – Agurain/Salvatierra loop from Améscoa Baja&lt;/a&gt;, can take nearly 5 hours.
tips: The huts are the typical Navarran houses that belong to the shepherds of the area.

Within the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park is this wonderful place with an interesting history related to its name. In addition to being one of the most spectacular caves in the area, its name dates back to the Carlist era in which it is said that General Zumalacarregui threw the shot soldiers of the other side, who were known as Cristinos or Isabelinos.
Before going to the place, I recommend that you check on the official website if access is open, a while ago it was closed due to COVID (http://cuevacristinos.com/)
Enchanted Forest of Artea is one of the many treasures that the Sierra de Urbasa hides. Labyrinthine karst rock formations.
This fresh water point is a treasure on days when the heat is stifling.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Zudaire, Larraona, Enchanted Forest of Artea – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route is approximately 42.6 km long and typically takes around 3 hours and 28 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied cycling experience. Approximately 20.6 km is asphalt, 9.02 km is paved, and a significant 9.74 km consists of unpaved sections. Cyclists should be prepared for potentially rougher terrain on the unpaved parts, which could include gravel, dirt, or uneven paths, requiring a bike suitable for mixed surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Zudaire, Larraona, Enchanted Forest of Artea – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can experience several notable points. These include the traditional shepherd houses known as Bordas, the intriguing Cueva de Los Cristinos, and the captivating Enchanted Forest of Artea. There's also a Fresh water spring available.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult. It features significant climbs and varied terrain, including unpaved sections, making it more suitable for experienced touring cyclists looking for a physical challenge rather than beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery, especially within the Enchanted Forest of Artea, is particularly vibrant or colorful. Summer can be hot, and winter might present challenging conditions due to cold or precipitation on the unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling this route, especially within Urbasa-Andia Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As this route passes through Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, it's important to be aware of park regulations. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within the park. However, always check the official Urbasa-Andia Natural Park website for the most current information regarding any specific regulations or temporary restrictions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Zudaire, Larraona, Enchanted Forest of Artea – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route, you can typically start and park in one of the villages along the route, such as Zudaire or Larraona. Look for public parking areas within these villages. Specific dedicated trailhead parking is not detailed, so village parking is usually the most practical option.

faqs.question: Is the Zudaire, Larraona, Enchanted Forest of Artea – Circular Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While cycling with dogs can be challenging on difficult routes, especially those with varied terrain and significant elevation, dogs are generally permitted in Urbasa-Andia Natural Park. If you choose to bring your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, physically capable of the distance and difficulty, and kept on a leash, especially in natural areas and near wildlife. Always carry waste bags and sufficient water for your pet.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers portions of the [CIMA NA11] Urbasa * Alsasua (16%) and [CIMA NA11] Urbasa * Olazagutia (16%) trails. You will also encounter smaller segments of paths like Artzaintza naturbidea, Camino de Larraona, and Camino de Iguru.

faqs.question: What kind of bicycle is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's 'difficult' classification and the significant unpaved sections (nearly 10 km), a touring bicycle with wider tires or a gravel bike would be most suitable. These types of bikes can handle the varied surfaces, from asphalt to rougher dirt and gravel paths, providing a more comfortable and safer ride.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through villages like Zudaire and Larraona, you may find local cafes, restaurants, or small shops for refreshments and supplies. It's advisable to check opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak season, and to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

waypoints.name: Bordas, the traditional houses of the local shepherds
